description: skills/bober.diagnose/SKILL.md adapts the 4-phase systematic-debugging discipline to incident investigation (system-level not code-level). Phases: Reproduce/Confirm → Gather Evidence at Boundaries → Hypothesize-and-Test → Verify Resolution. Designed for use by bober-diagnoser. Cross-references bober.debug for code-level overlap.

successCriteria.description: skills/bober.diagnose/SKILL.md exists with YAML frontmatter (name: bober-diagnose, description starts with 'Use when investigating a production incident or system-level failure'). MIT attribution to obra/superpowers as structural source.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify YAML and attribution.

successCriteria.description: Iron Law: 'NO REMEDIATION WITHOUT VERIFIED ROOT CAUSE AT TWO INDEPENDENT BOUNDARIES' (or equivalent — must require multi-source confirmation of root cause before suggesting fix). Red Flags list >=6 entries. Rationalization-Prevention table >=6 rows.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep file for the Iron Law structure and count entries.

successCriteria.description: Four phases documented in order with explicit gate language between each: Phase 1 Reproduce/Confirm, Phase 2 Gather Evidence at Boundaries, Phase 3 Hypothesize-and-Test, Phase 4 Verify Resolution. Each phase has a 'BEFORE proceeding to Phase N+1' gate.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read each phase. Verify gate language between phases.

successCriteria.description: Phase 1 includes: confirm symptom is current (not stale), confirm scope (one user / many / all), confirm timing (when did it start, has it changed in severity), record initial state to .bober/incidents/<id>/observations.jsonl.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read Phase 1. Verify all four sub-requirements documented.

successCriteria.description: Phase 2 includes: enumerate component boundaries (e.g., client→CDN→loadbalancer→service→database→storage), gather evidence AT each boundary from observability MCPs, correlate timestamps with .bober/incidents/<id>/changelog.jsonl (recent deploys / changes).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read Phase 2. Verify boundary enumeration discipline + observability MCP use + timestamp correlation.

successCriteria.description: Phase 3 includes: formulate hypotheses ranked by evidence, ACTIVELY seek contradicting evidence, propose tests that DISPROVE the top hypothesis. 'A hypothesis you cannot disprove is not strongly tested.'
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read Phase 3. Verify the disproof-seeking discipline.

successCriteria.description: Phase 4 includes: define resolution-verification criteria BEFORE remediation (e.g., 'error rate < 0.1% sustained for 10 minutes'), apply remediation via bober-deployer (Sprint 20) with checkpoint gates, monitor against criteria via observability MCPs, mark resolved only when criteria met for the named window.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read Phase 4. Verify the explicit pre-defined criteria requirement.

successCriteria.description: Cross-references: bober.debug (code-level overlap), bober.runbook (Sprint 18, for runbook-driven flows), bober.deploy (Sprint 20, for remediation execution), .bober/anti-patterns/ catalog.

generatorNotes:
Adapt /tmp/superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md (the 4-phase structure) to incident context. Sprint 2 already ported it for code-level bugs (bober.debug). THIS skill is the system-level twin.

Key divergences from bober.debug:
- Phase 1 is REPRODUCE+CONFIRM, not just reproduce — incidents can be transient or stale.
- Phase 2 is GATHER AT BOUNDARIES — emphasizes the multi-component nature of production systems. Adapt the 'Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems' subsection from the source SKILL.md.
- Phase 3 includes ACTIVELY SEEK CONTRADICTING EVIDENCE — superpowers's source mentions this; emphasize it more for incidents because confirmation bias under pressure is the dominant failure mode.
- Phase 4 adds RESOLUTION VERIFICATION CRITERIA with metric+window — code bugs are 'tests pass'; incidents are 'metric returned to baseline'.

Gate language between phases (verbatim style):
```
<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
BEFORE proceeding to Phase 2, you MUST have completed Phase 1 in writing — symptom confirmed current, scope confirmed, timing confirmed, observations.jsonl appended. If any of these is incomplete, return to Phase 1. Skipping Phase 1 makes Phase 2 evidence-gathering ungrounded.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
```

Observation log shape (.bober/incidents/<id>/observations.jsonl):
```
{"timestamp": "...", "phase": 1, "observation": "Symptom: 500 errors on /api/checkout from 14:00 UTC", "source": "user-report", "verified": true}
```

Resolution-verification criteria example for Phase 4 (in the skill body as a worked example):
```
Resolution criteria for INC-2026-0524-001:
  - Metric: api.checkout.error_rate
  - Threshold: < 0.1%
  - Window: 10 minutes sustained
  - Comparison baseline: 7-day rolling average
  - Verification source: obs__datadog__query_metric
```

Length: 200-300 lines. Verbatim voice (per spec directive) — capitalized rules, all-caps STOP, <EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT> tags.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify gate language between phases is ACTUALLY GATING — language like 'consider doing X before Y' is not a gate. Required language: 'BEFORE proceeding to Phase N+1, you MUST have done X'. Without this strict framing the phases collapse into suggestions.

Verify Phase 4's resolution-verification criteria are PRE-DEFINED (before remediation), not retrofitted after. The skill must explicitly forbid 'mark resolved because the dashboard looks better' — that's the very anti-pattern this skill exists to prevent.

Verify hypothesis-disproof discipline in Phase 3 is REQUIRED not advisory. 'Try to find evidence that DISPROVES your top hypothesis. A hypothesis you cannot disprove is not strongly tested.' or equivalent — must appear as a step, not a suggestion.

Cross-check: bober.debug (Sprint 2) and bober.diagnose (this sprint) should be DIFFERENT enough to justify both existing. bober.debug = code-level, sprint-evaluator-feedback-driven; bober.diagnose = system-level, incident-driven. If the two skills are 80% the same, consolidate them (rare; usually they diverge naturally). Read both to confirm distinct value.

Verify the verbatim-voice directive holds: <EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT> tags should appear at least at phase transitions. Iron Law in capitalized fenced block.

Verify .bober/incidents/ schema references are CONSISTENT with what Sprint 19 will create — phases reference observations.jsonl, changelog.jsonl. Sprint 19 must use the same names.
